title.emphasis: 1. The Contractual Framework: A Unilateral Offer to the World

section.para: The governing agreement for any social media platform is its Terms of Service (ToS), a classic contract of adhesion that users must accept to participate.1 While some legal scholars have analyzed these as complex bilateral or "networked" contracts, the core transaction of content creation aligns more precisely with the structure of a unilateral contract: a promise made in exchange for the performance of an act.3

listitem.para: The Offer: The platform's offer is, in essence: "If you create content that adheres to our rules (as defined by our ToS, Community Guidelines, and moderation policies) and upload it using our interface, we will host it and make it available to others." This is a one-sided promise that becomes active only when the other party completes the specified task.6

Acceptance by Performance: The user does not enter into a bilateral negotiation or exchange mutual promises to create specific content. Instead, they accept the platform's standing offer each time they perform the act of creating and uploading a post, photo, or video.7 The contract is formed only upon the completion of this performance.6

Consideration: The consideration exchanged is multifaceted. The user receives access to the platform and potential visibility. The platform, in turn, receives a broad, perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free, and sublicensable license to the user's IP, which is the essential raw material for its advertising-based business model.9

section.para: This unilateral framework is crucial because it defines the relationship not as a partnership, but as a task-based exchange. The platform sets the terms, and the user performs the task to receive the benefit.
